examples.title: Sizeof Operator Demonstration
examples.description: Program to demonstrate the use of sizeof operator with different data types
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates the sizeof operator which returns the size of data types in bytes. It shows that on most systems, char is 1 byte, int is 4 bytes, float is 4 bytes, and double is 8 bytes. The %zu format specifier is used for printing size_t values.

examples.title: Type Casting Examples
examples.description: Program demonstrating both implicit and explicit type casting in C
examples.explanation: This program shows type casting: implicit casting happens automatically (integer division result assigned to float), while explicit casting uses (type) operator. Character constants are automatically promoted to int in expressions. The cast truncates the decimal portion when converting from float/double to int.

examples.title: Enumerated Data Type Demo
examples.description: Program demonstrating the use of enum for creating named constant values
examples.explanation: The enum keyword creates a user-defined type of integer constants. By default, the first constant is 0, and subsequent values increment by 1. Custom values can be assigned. Enums improve code readability by using meaningful names instead of magic numbers.

examples.title: Constants in C
examples.description: Program demonstrating the use of const keyword and #define macro
examples.explanation: This program shows two ways to create constants: #define (preprocessor macro) and const keyword. const provides type checking and scope control, while #define is a simple text replacement. The const approach is generally preferred in modern C programming for better type safety and debugging capabilities.
